dimensions.name: High-intent searchers
dimensions.logic: Users already comparing options convert with the least friction.
dimensions.objection: Search volume may be too small to spend the allocated budget.

dimensions.name: Ad-fatigued cohort
dimensions.proxy: frequency > 6 in 7 days with negative feedback
dimensions.logic: Suppression target, not an acquisition audience.
dimensions.objection: None — monitor that frequency caps trigger in time.

platforms.name: Search Ads
platforms.summary: Captures users already searching the category.
platforms.execute: Brand + category + comparison campaigns; keep a 5% brand-term holdout.
platforms.consumerLanguage: Ad copy in the buyer's own words.
platforms.risk: Brand-term conversions would partly happen organically.

platforms.name: Retargeting
platforms.summary: Warm visitors have the lowest marginal acquisition cost.
platforms.execute: Frequency-capped sequences with a 10% no-touch control group.
platforms.consumerLanguage: Evidence recap for visitors who did not buy.

messageContexts.say: Answer the buyer question with the core claim and its proof.
messageContexts.claimBoundary: Only claims backed by the proof packet.

messageContexts.buyerQuestion: Why should I come back now?
messageContexts.say: Re-present the strongest proof to deep visitors who did not buy.

treatments.name: Search high-intent capture
treatments.hypothesis: Evidence-first landing pages convert comparison searchers at acceptable incremental CAC.
treatments.message: Core claim answered with proof on the first screen.
risks: Brand terms convert organically; without a holdout the ad contribution is overstated.

treatments.name: Retargeting guardrail
treatments.hypothesis: Warm visitors convert at materially lower CAC than cold traffic.
treatments.message: Proof recap for evidence-page visitors; availability note for cart abandoners.
risks: Over-frequency in a sensitive category amplifies negative sentiment.

budgets.name: Search
budgets.action: Launch campaign structure with the brand-term holdout before scaling.

budgets.name: Retargeting
budgets.rationale: Protects the ROI floor at the lowest marginal cost.
budgets.action: Set frequency caps and the no-touch control before launch.

kol.name: Category creator pool — needs sourcing
kol.reason: Third-party proof content for the core claim.
kol.pricingBasis: Per-candidate: audience country share, average reach, usage rights.
kol.avatarSource: Neutral placeholder — candidates not sourced yet.

K01.avatarSource: Neutral placeholder — candidates not sourced yet.

reviewerChallenges.challenge: Search volume may be too thin to absorb 80% of the budget.
reviewerChallenges.response: Cap daily spend until impression share data confirms headroom.

assets.note: Replace with real platform icons (inline SVG or cached files). Remote URLs degrade to assets.fallback letter badges via the template's onerror chain.

kolVerifyDefault: Average views/reach of the last 10 posts
Usage rights and paid amplification permission
measurementRules: Scale budget only when the incremental CAC guardrail holds
Pause scaling when CAC exceeds the unit-margin safety line without long-term value evidence
Reduce reach when ad fatigue shows hides, negative feedback, or excess frequency
